channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: World's oldest man has died in England at 112
item.description: John Alfred Tinniswood was born a few months after the sinking of the Titanic and lived through two world wars.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: A new chancellor is elected for Oxford University's 800-year-old post
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Former U.K. Conservative Party leader William Hague has been elected chancellor of Oxford University, one of the most prestigious positions in British academia, the university said Wednesday. Hague was elected in an online vote by staff and alumni of the 800-year-old university.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: ICC prosecutor requests arrest warrants for the head of Myanmar's military regime
item.description: THE HAGUE (AP) — The International Criminal Court’s chief prosecutor asked judges on Wednesday to issue an arrest warrant for the head of Myanmar’s military regime for crimes committed against the country’s Rohingya Muslim minority. Senior Gen.
item.dc:creator: MOLLY QUELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What to know about the ceasefire deal between Israel and Lebanon's Hezbollah
item.description: A ceasefire deal that went into effect on Wednesday could end more than a year of cross-border fighting between Israel and Lebanon's Hezbollah militant group, raising hopes and renewing difficult questions in a region gripped by conflict. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: ADAM GELLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel and Lebanon's Hezbollah start a ceasefire after nearly 14 months of fighting
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israel and Lebanon-based Hezbollah militants began a ceasefire Wednesday in a major step toward ending nearly 14 months of fighting as a region on edge wondered whether it will hold.
item.dc:creator: JOSEF FEDERMAN, KAREEM CHEHAYEB and BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Namibia votes and could have its first female leader. But election upsets have shaken the region
item.description: A woman who joined Namibia’s independence movement in the 1970s is a strong contender to become its first female leader as the country votes in a presidential election.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Biggest November snowstorm in half century hits Seoul and grounds flights
item.description: The biggest November snowstorm to hit South Korea’s capital in more than a half century has blanketed the capital, grounding hundreds of flights and disrupting traffic.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: G7 ministers throw support behind Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire but make no mention of ICC warrant
item.description: FIUGGI, Italy (AP) — Foreign ministers from leading industrialized countries threw their strong support Tuesday behind an immediate ceasefire between Israel and the Lebanese militant group Hezbollah but sidestepped the question of whether to enforce an international arrest warrant for Israel’s leade
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Philippine investigators summon VP Duterte over her public threats against President Marcos
item.description: MANILA, Philippines (AP) — Philippine authorities handed a subpoena to Vice President Sara Duterte’s office Tuesday, inviting her to answer investigators' questions after she publicly threatened to have the president, his wife and the House of Representatives speaker assassinated if she were killed
item.dc:creator: JIM GOMEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Australia's social media ban for kids is closer to becoming law
item.description: Australia’s House of Representatives has passed a bill that would ban children younger than 16 years from social media.
item.dc:creator: Rod Mcguirk, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: A fugitive wanted in the US for a pair of bombings is arrested in the UK after 20 years on the run
item.description: LONDON (AP) — A suspected animal rights extremist wanted in the U.S. for bombings in the San Francisco area was arrested in Britain after more than 20 years on the run from the law, officials said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN MELLEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden to make new push for ceasefire in Gaza after Israel-Hezbollah deal
item.description: President Biden on Tuesday said the U.S. will make a new push for a ceasefire in Gaza in the coming days, after Israel and Hezbollah agreed to pause their fighting in and around the border with Lebanon.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: Israel, Lebanon's Hezbollah agree to a ceasefire to end nearly 14 months of fighting
item.description: Israel approved a United States-brokered ceasefire agreement with Lebanon's Hezbollah on Tuesday, setting the stage for an end to nearly 14 months of fighting linked to the ongoing war in the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden announces cease-fire plan between Israel and Hezbollah ending 14 months of fighting
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu announced his support for a cease-fire deal that has yet to be finalized between Israel and Hezbollah terrorists.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ukraine says Russian attack sets a new record for the number of drones used
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russia launched 188 drones against most regions of Ukraine in a nighttime blitz, the Ukrainian air force said Tuesday, describing it as a record number of drones deployed in a single attack.
item.dc:creator: HANNA ARHIROVA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mexican cattle imports suspended after New World screwworm detected
item.description: The United States Department of Agriculture’s Animal and Plant Health Inspection Service (APHIS) announced that a New World screwworm (NWS) was detected in Mexico on Friday, causing the agency to pause animal imports from the country. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Philippine president and vice president clash in a feud that's testing an Asian democracy
item.description: MANILA, Philippines (AP) — A major political storm set off by a bitter feud between the Philippine president and the vice president is testing an Asian democracy that has long been troubled by rowdy politics, poor law enforcement and bareface impunity.
item.dc:creator: JIM GOMEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Germany's Merkel recalls Putin's 'power games' and contrasting US presidents in her memoirs
item.description: Former German Chancellor Angela Merkel recalls Vladimir Putin’s “power games” over the years, remembers contrasting meetings with Barack Obama and Donald Trump and says she asked herself whether she could have done more to prevent Brexit.
item.dc:creator: Geir Moulson, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Bah, humbug! Vandal smashes Ebenezer Scrooge's tombstone used in 'A Christmas Carol' movie
item.description: If life imitates art, a vandal may be haunted by The Ghost of Christmas Yet to Come for smashing a tombstone that marked the fictional grave of Ebenezer Scrooge in the English countryside.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Israel 'moving forward' on possible Hezbollah cease-fire, official says
item.description: The Israeli Security Cabinet will convene Tuesday to discuss a possible cease-fire agreement with Hezbollah terrorists in Lebanon, Ambassador Danny Danon said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russia tricks Yemeni men to fight in Ukraine under Houthi scheme
item.description: Russia has begun luring Yemeni men to fight in its war in Ukraine by falsely promising lucrative jobs before forcing them to sign military contracts and then sending them to fight on the front lines.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hezbollah fires barrage of rockets into Israel after IDF targets Hezbollah command centers in Beirut
item.description: Hezbollah on Sunday fired missiles and other projectiles into Israel in retaliation for to deadly IDF strikes on the militant group’s command centers in Beirut.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Drones detected near 3 US bases in England last week: Air Force
item.description: Between Wednesday and Friday, multiple unidentified drones were detected near RAF Lakenheath, RAF Mildenhall and RAF Feltwell.
item.dc:creator: Elina Shirazi, NewsNation

item.title: Man in India regains consciousness before his cremation on funeral pyre: reports
item.description: A 25-year-old man who was declared dead in India regained consciousness shortly before he was set to be cremated this week, according to reports.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Scientists study ‘very rare’ frozen remains of 35,000-year-old saber-toothed cub
item.description: Scientists studied a three-week-old saber-toothed cub dating back 35,000 that had been left almost perfectly intact, thanks to its frozen condition.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel ambassador nominee Mike Huckabee: Hostages must be released
item.description: Israel ambassador nominee Mike Huckabee criticized the U.S. on Friday for pressuring Israel instead of holding Hamas accountable.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation Staff, NewsNation

item.title: A look inside the most expensive house in the world — the home of the UK’s monarch
item.description: Buckingham Palace in London is worth about $4.9 billion and has about 775 rooms, including royal and guest rooms, offices and bathrooms.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Death toll in Gaza from Israel-Hamas war passes 44,000, Palestinian officials say
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — The death toll in the Gaza Strip from the 13-month-old war between Israel and Hamas has surpassed 44,000, local health officials said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and FATMA KHALED, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Netanyahu ‘in very bad company’ with ICC indictment: William Cohen
item.description: The ICC issued arrest warrants for Netanyahu and a former defense minister on war crimes allegations.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: Top war-crimes court issues arrest warrants for Netanyahu and others in Israel-Hamas fighting
item.description: THE HAGUE (AP) — The world’s top war-crimes court issued arrest warrants Thursday for Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu, his former defense minister and Hamas’ military chief, accusing them of crimes against humanity in connection with the 13-month war in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: MOLLY QUELL, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Ukraine says Russia launched an intercontinental missile in an attack for the first time in the war
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said a missile used “matches the speed and altitude” of an ICBM. The Kremlin refused to comment on the strike.
item.dc:creator: Hanna Arhirova, WGN 9

item.title: Admiral: Middle East, Ukraine conflicts strain US air defense stocks
item.description: Conflicts in the Middle East and Ukraine are adding a strain to the U.S. military’s ability to respond to China, a top U.S. admiral warns.
item.dc:creator: Tanya Noury, NewsNation
